data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== gap analysis: our set vs DSO brainstorm + preplanning
=== agent adefcfb3b1d5d369f

I have now ground-truthed every high-priority surface the user flagged. I have enough to produce the gap analysis. Let me compile the findings.

The key insight from the inference-signal scan (Part B) and behavioral-prohibition mode (Part C) is critical: these are plan-as-built review mechanics our set only partially covers. Let me now write the complete gap analysis.

---

# Adversarial GAP ANALYSIS: DSO brainstorm/preplanning plan-review criteria vs. our rebar gate dimension set

I ground-truthed every flagged surface against actual DSO source (not just the catalog): the 4 fidelity reviewers (`agent-clarity.md`/`scope.md`/`value.md`/`feasibility-reviewer.md`), the Understanding Gate (`brainstorm/SKILL.md`), the 6-reviewer architecture board (`preplanning/docs/reviewers/*.md`), the red-team agent (`red-team-reviewer.md`), the DD-quality micro-checks + scripts (`check-dd-lexicon.sh`, `spec-fidelity-check.sh`, `story-decomposer.md`), and the inline gap analysis (`epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md`). The catalog was accurate; corrections noted below are mine where source diverged.

**Verdict up front: the user's instinct is correct.** Our set covers the *spine* (intent fidelity, scope accuracy, measurability, child coverage, the architecture-board overlays). But it is **missing or under-specifying ~12 distinct plan-as-built review behaviors**, concentrated in five clusters: (1) the **inference/assumption-sourcing audit**, (2) **SC→DD coverage as a structured bucket-audit with a 3-condition standard**, (3) the **DD-quality lexical micro-checks**, (4) **provenance + state-lifecycle ownership**, and (5) several **architecture-board sub-dimensions** our T5 overlays flatten away. Details below.

---

## 1. GAPS — DSO evaluates this; our set does NOT

### G-A. Inference/assumption-sourcing audit (the single biggest gap)
**DSO:** Two independent mechanisms. (a) Brainstorm **Inference-Signal Scan** (`epic-scrutiny-pipeline.md:65-84`): scans the plan's Inputs/Approach narrative for hedging ("assume/likely/should be") and confident-assertion ("loaded from/provided by/comes from/reads from") trigger words, marks each as `<<inferred:source>>`, and fires a **MUST-execute Read/Grep** to confirm the assumed input source actually exists — with an explicit prohibition on reasoning from cached context (`:82`). (b) Red-team **inference_challenge mode** (`red-team-reviewer.md:14-124`): challenges plan decisions that were *inferred without explicit user sourcing*, sampled 100% for high-stakes fields.
**Why it matters:** This is the structural defense against the plan silently assuming an input/source/dependency exists. It is *exactly* the U8 sycophancy / U2 fault-localization failure class, but DSO catches it via a **trigger-word→forced-verification** loop, not a general read.
**Recommendation:** **ADD as new dim** (call it `E9 assumption-source verification`). Our E4 (premise soundness) and P2/P3 (reference resolution) are adjacent but neither does the *hedge/assertion-phrase scan that forces a codebase probe on the named source*. This is cheap, deterministic-trigger + agent-verify, and squarely in-scope for a review process. Adopt as-is — no constraint violation.

### G-B. SC→DD coverage as a 4-bucket structured audit with the explicit 3-condition standard
**DSO:** `red-team-reviewer.md:150-175` + `story-decomposer.md:201-208`. Every SC is bucketed into exactly one of `fully_covered` / `partially_covered` / `uncovered` / `out_of_scope_for_stories`, and a DD "covers" an SC **only when all three hold: (1) same observable outcome, (2) same/wider scope, (3) same measurability.** A mandatory `sc_coverage_summary` audit trail is emitted regardless of findings.
**Why it matters:** This is the anti-summarization-drift defense — the #1 way decomposition silently weakens a parent criterion. Our **G3 child coverage** says "3-condition standard" in a parenthetical but does **not** enumerate the three conditions, does not require the per-SC bucket verdict, and does not mandate the audit-trail emission.
**Recommendation:** **FOLD into G3, but materially upgrade it.** G3 must (i) enumerate the 3 conditions verbatim, (ii) require a per-parent-criterion bucket verdict (covered/partial/uncovered/structural), (iii) emit the coverage map as a required output even on PASS. As written, G3 is a one-liner where DSO has a rigorous protocol — this is a PARTIAL masquerading as covered. In-scope; adopt as-is.

### G-C. DD-quality lexical micro-checks (compound / vague / spec-fidelity)
**DSO:** Three deterministic checks enforced by scripts, halting story finalization:
- **compound** (`check-dd-lexicon.sh:22-31`): flags the literal uppercase ` AND ` joining two requirements in one DD → must split.
- **vague** (`check-dd-lexicon.sh:33-56`): case-insensitive whole-word match against a **9-word banned list** (`properly, correctly, appropriately, well, adequate, reasonable, appropriate, suitable, should`). (Correction: the catalog's "properly/correctly/should" is illustrative; the enforced list is 9 words. `good/better/clean` are in a stale comment, NOT enforced.)
- **spec-fidelity ≥80%** (`spec-fidelity-check.sh`): each parent PIL criterion must have ≥80% of its words present in some DD line (set-overlap, normalized) or it's a dropped-criterion FAIL.
**Why it matters:** These are *cheap deterministic* AC/DD-quality gates. Our **F1** (measurability) is an LLM judgment that an AC is "observable pass/fail" — it does NOT catch compound conjunctions or run a vague-word lexicon, and our set has **nothing** like the spec-fidelity word-overlap drop-detector.
**Recommendation:** **ADD to TIER 0 (DET)** as a new `P6 AC/DD lexical quality` check: compound-conjunction scan + vague-word lexicon + (optionally) spec-fidelity word-overlap against parent criteria. These are pure-deterministic, fail-open on non-text, and align perfectly with our "DET prefilter → cheap → AGENT" ladder. The spec-fidelity drop-check partly overlaps G-B/E3 but is a distinct, near-free signal. Adopt as-is.

### G-D. Provenance classification of SCs/claims
**DSO:** `brainstorm/SKILL.md:565-577` — every SC and key context claim is tagged with a 5-class provenance: `explicit` / `confirmed-via-gap-question` / `inferred` / `researched` / `injected` (from cross-epic scan), with inferred sources wrapped `<<inferred:...>>` and rendered bold at the approval gate.
**Why it matters:** Provenance is *the* signal that lets a reviewer (and the approver) see which parts of the plan rest on unconfirmed inference vs. user-stated fact — it's the input to the inference-challenge. Our set has no notion of claim provenance.
**Recommendation:** **ADOPT-WITH-MODIFICATION / partially OUT-OF-SCOPE.** The `confirmed-via-gap-question` class presumes an interactive dialog we explicitly don't run. But a *reduced* provenance signal — flag SCs/claims that are **inferred or researched but unverified against the codebase** — is in-scope and feeds G-A. Recommend folding the *useful residue* into G-A (verify inferred/researched claims) rather than adding a full 5-class tagger.

### G-E. State-Lifecycle Owner audit (CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE)
**DSO:** `brainstorm/SKILL.md:579-589` — for any shared state the plan touches (CI var, marker file, lock, env var consumed by other tools, event class), a required table with one row per shared-state variable and four columns CREATE/UPDATE/CONSUME/RETIRE, each naming the specific owner. **RETIRE must always be filled**; an unowned RETIRE row is itself a defect.
**Why it matters:** This is the durable-state-leak defense — plans that introduce shared state nobody retires. Our **G1+G2** (edit-set/scope accuracy) finds *consumers of edited files* but does **not** audit lifecycle ownership of *shared mutable state* (the RETIRE dimension specifically has no analog anywhere in our set).
**Recommendation:** **ADD as a triggered overlay** under TIER 3 (`T9 shared-state lifecycle`): trigger when the plan introduces/mutates shared state (marker files, env vars, locks, CI vars, event types — rebar itself has these). Judge whether CREATE/CONSUME/RETIRE ownership is accounted for. In-scope; adopt as-is. (Note: highly relevant to rebar's own domain — session-log pointers, TAG_DELTA event classes, push markers.)

### G-F. The red-team 7-category interaction taxonomy is under-enumerated in our G4
**DSO:** `red-team-reviewer.md:186-240` — seven *named, defined* cross-story interaction failure modes: `implicit_shared_state`, `conflicting_assumptions`, `dependency_gap`, `scope_overlap`, `ordering_violation`, `consumer_impact`, `residual_references`. Each has a precise detection definition (e.g., `residual_references` = renames/relocates a shared resource but fails to enumerate ALL callers; `consumer_impact` = changes a contract without verifying downstream consumers still work).
**Why it matters:** Our **G4 child consistency** lists "shared-state/conflicting-assumptions/ordering/overlap/residual-refs" — that's **5 of 7**, and it **omits `dependency_gap`** (consumes another's output with no declared dependency) and **`consumer_impact`** (the operational-readiness one). It also treats them as a loose list, not a typed taxonomy with per-category detection rules.
**Recommendation:** **FOLD into G4 but complete it to all 7** and give each a one-line detection rule. `dependency_gap` partly overlaps our F3 (dependency_aware, currently FLAGGED/deferred) — resolve that: dependency_gap among *children* belongs in G4; cross-ticket prereqs stay in F3. In-scope.

### G-G. `residual_references` / `consumer_impact` as *enumeration completeness*, not just locus
**DSO:** `red-team-reviewer.md:177-184` (consumer enumeration) + categories 6/7 — requires the plan to **enumerate ALL** callers/consumers/references before a rename/relocate/contract-change, and verifies downstream consumers still *work* (functional, not just present).
**Why it matters:** Our **G1+G2** checks "no missing consumers" via reference lookup — close, but DSO's bar is *exhaustive enumeration of references to a deprecated/renamed resource* and *consumer behavioral verification*, which is stronger than "the impact set matches the real set."
**Recommendation:** **PARTIAL → strengthen G1+G2** to explicitly cover the rename/deprecate/relocate case (enumerate all references to the old name/path) and the contract-change case (consumers still function). See PARTIAL section.

---

## 2. PARTIAL — covered in spirit, but DSO is more rigorous

### P-1. Feasibility: the CLI `--help` empirical-verification protocol
**Our T3** (integration feasibility) says "external API/dep vs docs+working examples; CLI --help." **DSO** (`feasibility-reviewer.md:52-67,104-127`) is far more rigorous: a **mandatory per-subcommand inventory** where every CLI subcommand the plan will call is enumerated with `assumed_flags`+`assumed_payload_shape`, then `<tool> <subcommand> --help` is run to *empirically* observe actual flags, and **any mismatch — even `--label` vs `--labels`** — is a **critical capability gap**. Plus a 4-way capability classification (Verified/Partially/Unverified/Contradicted), an **auth-HTTPS precondition** check (OAuth needs HTTPS callbacks → HTTP-only env = Contradicted), and a **critical-gap⇒time-boxed-spike** recommendation.
**Delta:** We name `--help` but don't mandate the subcommand inventory, the assumed-vs-actual diff, the "any mismatch = critical" rule, or the auth-HTTPS precondition. **Strengthen T3** to adopt the inventory+diff protocol and the auth-HTTPS sub-check. In-scope (agent runs `--help` in-session).

### P-2. Testing-plan completeness vs the Testing reviewer's 3 dims
**Our E5** assesses "behavioral + contractual coverage & change-detector avoidance." **DSO Testing reviewer** (`testing.md:25-27`) splits this into three scored dims: `user_journey_coverage` (success **AND failure** experiences; caller-facing failure contract), `boundary_scenarios` (interruptions, input diversity, size/volume, environment variations, malformed payloads), and `verifiable_outcomes` (observable pass/fail, user-visible not internal-state).
**Delta:** E5 collapses failure-path + boundary + verifiable-outcome into one. The **boundary_scenarios** dimension (non-Latin/very-long/empty inputs, navigation interruptions, oversized files) and the **failure-experience contract** are largely absent from E5 and only weakly present in T6 (UX non-happy-path). **Strengthen E5** to explicitly require failure-path + boundary coverage, or split boundary into T6. In-scope.

### P-3. Maintainability board: `changeability` and `coupling_risk` have no analog
**Our T-overlays** cover perf/reliability/security/a11y but the catalog's "T5a/b/c/d" list and T7(docs) **omit maintainability's `coupling_risk` and `changeability`** (`maintainability.md:24-25`): coupling_risk = new cross-component dependency unacknowledged/unmitigated; changeability = hardcoding rules/thresholds the domain is known to evolve (e.g., prompt templates as string literals with no extraction path). Our T7 captures `documentation` (the 3rd maintainability dim) but not the first two.
**Delta:** No coupling/changeability review dimension exists in our set. **ADD a Maintainability overlay** (coupling_risk + changeability) alongside the existing architecture-board overlays — DSO treats it as a peer of security/perf. In-scope.

### P-4. Reliability: the `blast_radius` tiebreaker semantics
**Our T5b** (Reliability) is named but the catalog gives no rubric. **DSO** (`reliability.md:46-56`) has a precise rule: blast_radius is a **tiebreaker that only LOWERS a score, never raises it** — score the failure mode's inherent severity first, apply the −1 (min 1) blast-radius adjustment **only if the failure mode already warrants below-4**. A trivial failure with wide blast radius does NOT become blocking.
**Delta:** This is exactly the anti-FP calibration we care about (high blast radius ≠ automatic fail — mirrors our G1+G2 "high blast radius is NOT a fail"). **Adopt the tiebreaker rule into T5b's rubric.** In-scope and directly supports our FP discipline.

### P-5. Security `access_classification`: ambiguity-is-the-failure framing
**Our T5c** (Security) is unspecified. **DSO** (`security.md:23`) frames the core failure as **undeclared access level** (reader can't tell if public was intentional or auth forgotten), not absence of auth — a *documented* public endpoint scores well; an *undeclared* one scores ≤2 regardless.
**Delta:** Adopt the "ambiguity, not absence" framing + the OWASP-category-required-on-findings rule into T5c. Minor but sharpens the dim. In-scope.

### P-6. INVEST "Small = one agent session" — we have G5 but not the explicit sizing predicate
**Our G5/P4** judge "too-big-to-one-shot / should-have-children." **DSO INVEST** (`preplanning/SKILL.md:648-650`) defines **Small explicitly as "one sub-agent session"** and pairs it with the **vertical-slice** test (functional value, not horizontal layers: "user can upload PDF and see results" good; "create DB schema" bad) and the **foundation/enhancement split** heuristic.
**Delta:** G5 has the sizing judgment but lacks (i) the explicit "one session" predicate as the sizing unit and (ii) the **vertical-slice-vs-horizontal-layer** test — a plan decomposed into technical layers rather than value slices is a defect G5 doesn't name. **Strengthen G5** with the vertical-slice test. The session-sizing unit aligns with our agent-in-session framing — adopt. In-scope.

### P-7. Backward-compat: expand-contract specificity
**Our T4** flags both unacknowledged breakage and gratuitous backward-compat (YAGNI). **DSO** adds the **expand-contract / expand-migrate-contract pattern** as the *named expected approach* for safe schema/contract changes (`safety.md:25`, impl-plan; reliability failover idempotency `reliability.md:24`). Our T4 is bidirectional but doesn't name expand-contract as the positive pattern to check for.
**Delta:** Minor — fold "is a phased expand-contract migration used where a breaking change is unavoidable?" into T4. In-scope.

---

## 3. COVERED — accounted for (brief)

- **F1** ↔ DSO `success_measurable` + session-verifiable + end-state litmus + outcomes-not-impl. **Strong match** (and our agent-completable-in-session framing correctly *tightens* DSO's, while correctly rejecting the post-sprint/90-day-adoption ACs DSO also rejects — `agent-clarity.md:28`, `value.md:25`).
- **F4** ↔ DSO `user_impact` (named beneficiary + problem). Correctly reduced to a content-check (not dialog).
- **E1** ↔ SC↔description coherence / artifact-contradiction (Part A). Covered.
- **E2** ↔ ambiguity 6-signal + scope-bullet validity ("verify whether/check if/TBD" → undercooked). Covered; note our E2's spike/research-step flag mirrors DSO's scope-bullet "Pending Investigation" rule.
- **E3** ↔ intent fidelity / blind-restate. **Stronger than DSO** in mechanism (2-step CoVe); DSO relies on `self_contained` + gap analysis. Covered.
- **E4** ↔ premise soundness / anti-sycophancy + blue-team filter. Covered (and the blue-team 4-criterion filter — actionable/real-interaction/distinct/high-confidence — matches our adjudicator filter well).
- **E6** ↔ verification/termination completeness (U5/U6). Covered.
- **P2/P3** ↔ reference resolution + package existence (the file-inspection-isn't-behavioral spirit of the verify-command negative-constraint list). **Partial overlap:** the DSO **verify-command negative-constraint list** (`story-decomposer.md:240`: grep/find/ls/cat/test -f… are invalid verify commands because they prove existence, not behavior) is a *DD-quality* check we should note — it belongs with E5/E6 (a DD whose only verify is file-inspection is non-behavioral). Worth a one-line add to E6.
- **G3/G4** ↔ child coverage/consistency — covered but **incomplete** (see G-B, G-F).
- **T1** ↔ prior-art/novel-architecture justification (matches `justification.md` divergence_justified: name-alt + concrete-limitation + measurable-advantage). Covered.
- **T2** ↔ critical-gap⇒spike (red→green probe). Covered.
- **T4** ↔ compat/destructiveness (partial, see P-7).
- **T5a/c/d** ↔ Performance/Security/Accessibility board reviewers. Covered as overlays (sub-dimension gaps noted in P-3/P-4/P-5).
- **T6** ↔ UX non-happy-path / ux-probe-set (criticality/non_happy_path/flow_entry_exit). Covered.
- **T7** ↔ documentation reviewer + doc-freshness. Covered (and our CLAUDE.md-bloat antipattern is a good addition DSO lacks).
- **T8** ↔ bot-psychologist 17-pt LLM-instruction taxonomy. Covered.
- **Complexity-gate G1-G6 (YAGNI/Rule-of-Three/Dependency/Scale/Profiling)** ↔ our T4 (YAGNI) + P4 (oversize) + T1 (justification). **Partial:** Rule-of-Three (<3 call sites for a new abstraction) and the Dependency ≤30-LOC cost/benefit gate are not cleanly homed — candidates to fold into T1/P4, but lower priority.

---

## Priority recommendations (what to actually add)

**Tier-1 (clear gaps, cheap, in-scope — add these):**
1. **G-A** Assumption-source verification (hedge/assertion phrase scan → forced codebase probe). New dim E9. *Highest value — directly attacks the sycophancy/fault-localization failure class via a deterministic trigger.*
2. **G-C** AC/DD lexical micro-checks (compound-AND, 9-word vague lexicon, spec-fidelity ≥80% word-overlap). New DET dim P6. *Near-free, deterministic, fail-open.*
3. **G-B** Upgrade G3 to the 4-bucket SC→DD audit with the verbatim 3-condition standard + mandatory coverage map.
4. **G-F** Complete G4 to all 7 named interaction categories (add dependency_gap + consumer_impact).

**Tier-2 (real gaps, slightly heavier):**
5. **G-E** Shared-state lifecycle overlay (CREATE/CONSUME/**RETIRE**) — new T9.
6. **P-3** Maintainability overlay (coupling_risk + changeability).
7. **P-1** Strengthen T3 with the CLI `--help` subcommand-inventory + assumed-vs-actual diff + auth-HTTPS precondition.
8. **P-2** Strengthen E5 with explicit failure-path + boundary-scenario coverage.

**Tier-3 (rubric refinements, adopt the calibration):**
9. **P-4** Blast-radius-only-lowers tiebreaker into T5b. **P-5** ambiguity-not-absence into T5c. **P-6** vertical-slice test into G5. **P-7** expand-contract into T4. **G-D** reduced provenance (flag unverified inferred/researched claims) folded into G-A.

**Adopt-with-modification / constraint flags:**
- **G-D** full 5-class provenance — the `confirmed-via-gap-question` class assumes interactive dialog → reduce to "unverified-inference flag."
- DSO `validation_signal` (`value.md:25`) and several SC rules reward **dogfooding/staged-rollout/7-day-monitor** validation — these **violate our agent-completable-in-session constraint**. Correctly, our F1 already rejects them. Do **not** import DSO's "post-deployment observation is supplementary" allowance — our gate must keep the harder line (AC must be agent-verifiable in-session). Flagged so it isn't accidentally adopted.

**Two catalog corrections found in source:** (1) the enforced vague-word lexicon is **9 words** (`properly/correctly/appropriately/well/adequate/reasonable/appropriate/suitable/should`); `good/better/clean` appear only in a stale script comment and are NOT enforced. (2) The verify-command negative-constraint list and LIVE-VERIFIED→integration-test mapping live in `agents/story-decomposer.md` (lines 240, 244-258), not `preplanning/SKILL.md:240/274`.
